expectations.text: Spec has all required sections: Problem, Solution, Scope, Design Decisions, Failure Modes, Risks, Alternatives, Verification
expectations.evidence: Found required headings at SPEC.md:12 (## Problem), 18 (## Solution), 22 (## Scope), 39 (## Design Decisions), 63 (### Failure Modes), 70 (## Risks & Open Questions), 78 (## Alternatives Considered), and 84 (## Verification).

expectations.text: No code fences (```) anywhere in the spec outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: No triple-backtick code fences appear in SPEC.md:1-119. There is no Implementation Notes section with code blocks.

expectations.text: No TypeScript interface/type definitions in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (SPEC.md:41-61) contains no TypeScript interface/type declarations or typed signatures (e.g., interface/type blocks, ': string', '=> Promise').

expectations.text: No JSON object literals or proto field listings in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions defines a structured payload field listing: 'The context payload will include: current tab ... metadata (uid/title/version/tags); panel inventory (id/title/type/description/query count); ...' at SPEC.md:45, which is a data-shape enumeration rather than purely behavioral prose.

expectations.text: Scope items describe capabilities/behaviors, not exact file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope items at SPEC.md:26-30 describe behaviors/capabilities and include no exact file paths (e.g., src/features/...) or file extensions (.ts/.tsx).

expectations.text: Spec is under 120 lines (excluding Implementation Notes appendix)
expectations.evidence: SPEC.md is 119 lines total (read output reports '(End of file - total 119 lines)').

expectations.text: Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold topic sentences that state decisions as facts
expectations.evidence: All substantive Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold decision sentences at SPEC.md:41, 43, 45, 47, 49, 51, 53, 55, 57, 59, 61 (11/11, >80%).

expectations.text: An implementer could start coding from this spec without asking questions
expectations.evidence: Core behavior is internally ambiguous: Scope requires confirmation for entering/exiting edit mode (SPEC.md:28) and Design Decisions says edit mode toggle is confirmation-gated (SPEC.md:57), but Risks/Open Questions asks whether both enter and exit should be gated or only enter (SPEC.md:76). This unresolved decision can block implementation details.

expectations.text: Design Decisions section contains decisions, not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions includes high-level decisions and module naming but no code fences, TS interfaces/types, JSON/object literals, exact line-number references, mechanism tables, or useEffect/useState implementation details. The AGENTS.md path at SPEC.md:41 is cited as existing convention reference, not a new implementation file prescription.

expectations.text: Failure modes state policy decisions, not just error types
expectations.evidence: Failure Modes entries at SPEC.md:65-68 each pair a scenario with a non-trivial recovery policy (e.g., truncation strategy, reject-vs-coerce variable policy, conflict handling on stale targets, non-throwing structured errors). This is >50% policy-level decision content.

expectations.text: Scope items describe capabilities and behaviors, not files or architecture
expectations.evidence: All In Scope bullets at SPEC.md:26-30 are behavioral capability statements (context registration, tools, confirmation policy, metric context sufficiency, prompt lifecycle) and do not prescribe files/components.

claims.claim: The proposed solution follows the same 3-step integration pattern as Alerts.
claims.evidence: Explicitly stated in Solution at SPEC.md:20.

claims.claim: Mutating dashboard tools require explicit user confirmation.
claims.evidence: Stated in Scope at SPEC.md:28 and Design Decisions at SPEC.md:57.

claims.claim: Edit-mode confirmation behavior is fully resolved.
claims.evidence: Open question remains at SPEC.md:76 about whether both enter/exit or only enter should be confirmation-gated.

suggestions.reason: Add an assertion for internal consistency/unresolved contradictions across sections. This spec contains a required behavior and an open question about the same behavior (SPEC.md:28 vs SPEC.md:76), which current assertions only partially catch via implementer_can_start.
Consider explicitly checking that no open questions remain for in-scope core tool behavior; this makes the assertion more discriminating and less subjective.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertion set is generally strong on structure and anti-leak constraints, but it could better detect unresolved requirement conflicts that make implementation ambiguous.
